evidences.title: Monad (MON) Mainnet Launch: Historic Coinbase Token Sale ...
evidences.content: MONAD In November 2025, the cryptocurrency market witnessed a historic milestone as Monad (MON) became the first token ever sold on Coinbase’s newly launched public token sale platform. With a $2.5 billion fully diluted valuation, $244 million in venture backing, and a mainnet launch scheduled for November 24, Monad represents one of the year’s most anticipated blockchain infrastructure projects. The token sale, which opened November 17 and runs through November 22, has already generated unprecedented retail interest, while MEXC’s pre-market trading offers early access to MON tokens ahead of the official listing. [...] ### Historic Platform Debut Monad’s selection as Coinbase’s inaugural token sale project signals a fundamental shift in how major crypto assets reach the market. Following Coinbase’s $375 million acquisition of Echo Launchpad in October 2025, the exchange developed a token sale platform designed to address long-standing fairness issues in crypto fundraising. Unlike traditional Initial Exchange Offerings (IEOs) that often favor whales and institutional players, Coinbase’s model prioritizes retail accessibility and equitable distribution. evidences.title: Monad Token Debuts on Coinbase at $3.9 Billion Fully Diluted ...
evidences.content: Monad’s native token MON successfully launched on Coinbase with a $3.9 billion fully diluted valuation (FDV), marking a significant milestone in the cryptocurrency market. The Layer-1 blockchain project completed its initial token offering on Coinbase’s new token sale platform before transitioning to public trading. The token sale exceeded expectations by raising $269 million from more than 85,800 participants across 70+ countries. This impressive participation level demonstrates strong investor confidence in Monad’s vision and technology, despite initial concerns about the pace of the sale. evidences.content: The sale is the first direct token offering hosted on Coinbase. It is open to participants in over 80 countries, including the U.S. Early-stage activity was steady but slower than expected. Analysts attribute this to investors waiting for final moments to commit larger funds. Currently, the sale sees an additional $693,500 added each hour. The public sale will close on 23 November at 10:00, just ahead of Monad’s mainnet launch on 24 November 2025. ## What Monad Offers Monad is a high-performance Layer 1 blockchain. It is fully compatible with the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM). It is designed to reach 10,000 transactions per second with near-instant finality. The platform focuses on three core pillars: [...] A temporary halt occurred in March 2025 when a supermajority was reached at an epoch boundary. The issue was resolved, and stability returned. In September 2025, Monad open-sourced its execution client under the GPL-3.0 license. Previously, in September 2024, the Discord server link was temporarily compromised but later secured. ## Why the Raise Matters The presale demonstrates robust market interest in high-performance Layer 1 solutions. evidences.title: [PDF] monad / mon token – coinbase token sales disclosure1
evidences.content: Sale: up to 7.5B MON (7.5% of initial total supply). Sale Price: $0.025 per MON. Implied Fully Diluted Value (FDV) of the Monad Network: $2.5 billion. 2 Determination of the Sale Price: Prior to the public sale on Coinbase token sales, there has been no public market for MON tokens. The price of $0.025 per MON token in the public sale is a fixed price based on a $2.5 billion fully diluted network valuation and an initial total supply of 100 billion MON tokens. The sale price was determined through a structured approach that considers several key factors, including market conditions, competitive positioning, and strategic objectives. This methodology ensures that the sale price reflects both the intrinsic value of the Monad network and broader market dynamics. The pricing strategy aims to [...] launch of the Monad network. The donation was made to cover expected expenditures of the Monad Foundation into 2026. evidences.title: Monad token climbs 46% after early dip as Coinbase-sale buyers ...
evidences.content: Advertisement Monad’s MON token traded around $0.0365 by mid-afternoon Monday, up roughly 46% from its $0.025 sale price after recovering from an early slide to about $0.02 shortly after tokens were distributed. It follows the close of Monad's week-long public token sale on Coinbase, the first conducted through the exchange’s new token-launch platform. The offering attracted $269 million in commitments from nearly 86,000 participants, a 1.43× oversubscription of the $187.5 million allocated amount. Monad Token (MON) Price Chart. Source: Coinbase Participants came from more than 70 countries, and Coinbase said internal polling showed most joined for long-term exposure rather than short-term speculation. [...] Coinbase's strategic acquisitions: What's driving the spree? 🚀 Since its founding in 2012, Coinbase has acquired dozens of companies, with 2025 alone marking eight such deals, including the recent $375M purchase of Echo, an on-chain investing platform. This acquisition trend isn't new for Coinbase; similar sprees occurred in 2018 during the "Great Crypto Crash" and in 2021 amid a crypto bull market. The standout fact? Coinbase is actively expanding its ecosystem to support crypto builders and fundraisers with acquisitions like Liquifi and Echo, ensuring innovation and growth despite past market volatility. The benefit? A more robust and democratized investment landscape for founders and communities alike. evidences.title: The ideal way to launch – introducing token sales on ...
evidences.content: "Filling up from the bottom": Our algorithm is designed to promote broader distribution and limit asset concentration among large purchasers. This will typically result in more complete allocation for participants requesting the lowest amounts while progressively filling larger requests until the supply is exhausted. Request window: Token sales hosted on Coinbase will be open for a finite amount of time (e.g., one week). Users can come in at any point during this window to submit their request for tokens. Once the window closes, our algorithm determines the final allocation for everyone. [...] Coinbase is providing access for the retail community across most regions globally at launch, with plans to expand in the future. For the first time since 2018, users in the United States will now be able to widely participate – a huge win for the American cryptoeconomy. We are hosting the first token sale from November 17-22; follow @Coinbase on X for more details. ### A New Standard for Token Sales Unlike "first-come, first-served" sales that fail to reach a project’s real community, our token sale design prioritizes the many over the few. evidences.title: Coinbase's Monad public token sale starts hot and then ...
evidences.content: Although arguably off to a lackluster start, the Monad token could still prove to be a seminal moment for not only Coinbase but also crypto as a whole. After last decade's initial coin offering, or ICO, boom attracted some fraudsters seeking to take advantage of investors, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission began cracking down in 2018. The SEC classified many tokens as unregistered securities, which effectively ended the ICO era and pushed most new token launches offshore or into private sales limited to accredited investors. [...] Coinbase launched its new public token sales platform shortly after buying Echo, the onchain fundraising platform founded by popular crypto trader Jordan Fish (aka Cobie). The U.S.-based cryptocurrency exchange has said it would launch a new token sale about once a month. Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data.
